curveRfreq 0.0.0.9000

    • Frequentist Modeling of Immunoassay Standard Curves with the StandardCurve Class: ELISA Example
    • Frequentist Modeling of Immunoassay Standard Curves with the StandardCurve Class: Bead Array Example

Check Consistency Between fixed_a_result and coef(fit)

Source: R/curve_fitting.R, R/error_propagation.R
check_fixed_a_fit_consistency.Rd

Validates that fixed_a_result and the free parameters in coef(fit) are mutually consistent: exactly one of (a) a is fixed and absent from coef(fit), or (b) a is free and present in coef(fit).

Call this just before propagate_error_dataframe to catch mismatches early.

Usage

check_fixed_a_fit_consistency(
  fit,
  fixed_a_result,
  context = "",
  verbose = TRUE
)

check_fixed_a_fit_consistency(
  fit,
  fixed_a_result,
  context = "",
  verbose = TRUE
)

Arguments

fit

Fitted model object with a coef() method.

fixed_a_result

Numeric scalar or NULL.

context

Character string describing the call site.

verbose

Logical; if TRUE emit informational messages (default TRUE).

Value

Named list with:

fixed_a_result

The (possibly corrected) fixed_a value.

consistent

Logical.

correctable

Logical. If FALSE propagation cannot proceed.

A named list with elements fixed_a_result, consistent, and correctable.

On this page
